title: oxalis polymorpha subsp. tijucana
intro: Oxalis polymorpha subsp. tijucana, also known as the Tijuca sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family, Oxalidaceae, native to Brazil. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Oxalis polymorpha subsp. tijucana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Oxalis polymorpha subsp. tijucana can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and stems can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
